title: Soy Curd Fruit Bowl
description: Unsweetened soy curd topped with seasonal fruit—a light main for breakfast, post-workout recovery, or evening snack. Contains soy; nut-free as written.
explanation: This is the soy-based fruit-and-curd path for recovery and breakfast. It is intentionally separate from peanut curd so soy-free and nut-free filters stay honest.
Use plain unsweetened soy curd. Sweetness should come from fruit, not sugar-heavy flavored yogurts.
ingredients: 1 tsp Flaxseed powder or Chia seeds (optional)
instructions: If soy curd is very thick or sour, whisk briefly until smooth. Divide into two bowls.
Top each bowl with chopped seasonal fruit.
Add optional lemon, cardamom or cinnamon, and flax or chia.
Garnish with mint. Eat soon after assembling.
stepNotes: Choose unsweetened soy curd; check the label for added sugar.
If curd is watery, drain in a sieve for 10 minutes for a thicker bowl.
Post-workout: eat within a comfortable window after training; no need to rush if appetite is low.
Do not mix peanut curd into this recipe—use a separate peanut-curd recipe if needed.
Skip optional seeds if you want the simplest fruit-and-curd bowl.

variationSections.title: Fruit toppings by season
items: Banana and papaya work most of the year.

variationSections.title: Texture upgrades
items: A spoon of granola only if gluten-free is not required.
Keep nuts out of the default bowl so nut-free stays accurate.
